commit.message:
Run real vLLM instances in disaggregated serving demo

Launch two vLLM servers (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct) as Prefill and Decode,
route requests through the proxy, and send test prompts end-to-end.
Demonstrates the full Mooncake proxy pattern with real LLM inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add vLLM disaggregated serving proxy for real Mooncake + vLLM deployment

Implements a simplified version of Mooncake's vllm_v1_proxy_server.py that
routes requests between vLLM Prefill (kv_producer) and Decode (kv_consumer)
instances. Supports round-robin load balancing across multiple nodes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: nano-mooncake — Mooncake minimal prototype in ~650 lines of Python

Implements the core design of Mooncake (FAST 2025 Best Paper) as a teaching prototype:
- Transfer Engine with pluggable TCP transport and batch async transfers
- Mooncake Store with Master/Client, two-phase put, replica tracking, and lease eviction
- HTTP metadata server for segment discovery
- Two runnable examples: Prefill/Decode disaggregation and distributed KVCache store

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: wrap file upload in try/catch to prevent stuck uploading state

If /api/upload returns non-JSON (e.g. Render 500 HTML error), res.json()
would throw and uploading/uploadingIdx state would stay true forever,
permanently disabling the upload button. Fixed in cart, academy, and admin
store pages using finally block to guarantee state is reset.

debug(model-3d): add diagnostic logging to arc face splitting

Temporary console.warn logging at every step of splitFaceByCurve and
finalizeArc to trace exactly where the failure occurs. Check browser
console for [splitFaceByCurve] and [ArcTool] messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fix arc curve splitting wrong edges on extruded box faces

Root cause: splitFace creates two faces where the split edge goes
endV→startV on faceA and startV→endV on faceB (due to index sorting).
The intermediate vertex code always used faceA to find the outgoing
edge from prevVertex, but on faceA the outgoing edge leads to a
BOUNDARY vertex (not vertexB). This caused splitEdge to split boundary
edges shared with adjacent side walls, corrupting box geometry.

Fix: detect which face (faceA or faceB) has the A→B split edge direction
by checking if the outgoing half-edge from vertexA leads to vertexB.
Use that face for the intermediate vertex loop.

Added test: splitFaceByCurve on a 6-face box verifies side walls are
not corrupted (max 5 vertices each) and all curve vertices remain coplanar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: plan renewal at $19 + remove commission on upgrades/renewals

- First activation (NONE → plan): full price + 20% sponsor commission
- Upgrade (BASIC→PRO etc.): differential price, NO commission
- Renewal (same plan): fixed $19 USD, NO commission, extends expiry

Changes:
- pack-requests: allow same-plan renewal at $19 (AppSetting PRICE_RENEWAL);
  block only downgrades; extend plan_expires_at on renewal CRYPTO path
- admin/purchases: allow renewal approval; extend expiry (not reset);
  commission only when currentRank === 0
- purchases/verify cron: detect renewal by plan match; extend vs set plan;
  commission only on first activation
- activate-plan: allow renewal; extend expiry; commission only from NONE

commit.message:
Fix menu right border alignment

printf pads by bytes; box-drawing chars (│, ─) are 3 bytes each but
1 display col, causing a 3-col shortfall. Increased padding widths
by 3 to compensate (%-52s→%-55s for groups, %-47s→%-50s for rows).

commit.message:
Fix 5x mastering slowdown — remove oversampling from measurement chain (v10.16.12)

measureChainLoudness had 4 waveshapers with '2x'/'4x' oversampling running
across full track duration × 4 LUFS passes. Oversampling doesn't affect LUFS
prediction — only aliasing quality which is irrelevant for measurement.

Changed all measurement waveshapers to oversample='none' and capped analysis
duration at 60s (was full track — 4+ min tracks rendered 4 complete passes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Express backend with Supabase, encrypted PRO credentials, and cron sync

- Express API with auth, PRO CRUD, and settings routes
- AES-256-GCM encryption for PRO account IDs (no more localStorage)
- node-cron scheduler to refresh royalty data every 6 hours
- Supabase schema with RLS policies for all tables
- Frontend contexts updated to use backend API
- Vite dev proxy for /api routes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2.1: two defensive fixes (TT bounds check + time floor)

- is_tt_move_valid(): add bounds check (piece > k → return 0) before
  bitboards[] access; prevents OOB read when Lazy SMP torn TT write
  produces piece field 12–15
- allocate_time(): fix panic-mode 50ms floor to be bounded by available
  time (my_time_ms > 50 ? 50 : my_time_ms/3); prevents timeout when
  remaining time < 50ms with no increment

Both fixes are search-neutral; perft 5 = 4865609 verified.

commit.message:
fix: skip native health modules in Expo Go, improve pedometer reliability

- Detect Expo Go via Constants.executionEnvironment and skip
  react-native-health / react-native-health-connect entirely
  (they hang or crash without native linking)
- Go straight to expo-sensors Pedometer in Expo Go (no 10s wait)
- Add timeout to Pedometer.isAvailableAsync() to prevent hanging
- Better error logging for pedometer failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix logo not loading: serve static files from SPA catch-all route

The SPA catch-all was returning index.html for all non-API paths including
image requests like /icon_blue.png. Now checks if a static file exists on
disk before falling back to the SPA HTML.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gamification): real streak tracking, heart animation, milestone toasts

- Streak now persists in localStorage (bs-streak key), increments on
  consecutive daily visits, resets if a day is missed
- Weekly calendar shows actual visited days of the current week instead
  of filling blindly from Sunday
- Today's day label highlighted in amber; unvisited today shown with
  amber ring so users know where they stand
- Streak milestone toasts on days 7, 14, 30, 50, 100 (shown once per
  milestone via bs-streak-milestone key)
- Heart pop animation (bsHeartPop keyframe) on love — scales up then
  settles, plays on StoryCard and StoryRail
- Love milestone toasts: "5 stories loved", "10 loves today", "25 loves"
- Session stats (stories read + loved) shown at the CaughtUp end-of-feed
  moment so users feel a sense of accomplishment
- Read count tracked per session via openStoryModal helper

commit.message:
fix: relax consensus guard in paper mode, enforce forced exploration

Paper/bootstrap mode needs trade data to learn. The consensus guard
was blocking ALL trades when <3 of 8 domains had signals (common in
strong trends). Now advisory-only in paper mode. Forced exploration
(>6h no trades) is now highest-priority override that cannot be
blocked by consensus guard, regime win rate, or calibration warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(launch): relay node, set-bootstrap script, v1.0.0

Step 1 — Relay node support:
- Add SWARM_RELAY_MODE=true setting to NodeSettings
- Add RelayNode class in main.py: runs P2P (discovery + heartbeat +
  metrics) without loading a model or training. Logs its own multiaddr
  prominently at startup so the operator can copy it into manifests.
- cli() dispatches to RelayNode when relay_mode=True, SwarmNode otherwise
- Add docker/docker-compose.relay.yml for one-command VPS deployment

Step 2 — Zero-config participant join:
- Add scripts/set_bootstrap.py: updates bootstrap_peers in all runs/*.json
  from a single multiaddr argument. Atomic write (tmp → rename).
- Add make relay-up, relay-down, relay-logs, set-bootstrap targets

Step 3 — Public release:
- Bump version 0.1.0 → 1.0.0 (triggers publish.yml on v1.0.0 tag)

110 tests passing. Lint + mypy --strict clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Textual-native file browser for Linux terminals

On Linux, native dialog tools (zenity, kdialog, yad, qarma) are often
absent and tkinter may be broken (missing libtk8.6.so), causing the
Browse button to silently return nothing.

Replace the Linux browse path with a built-in Textual modal:
- _VideoTree: DirectoryTree subclass filtered to dirs + supported video
  extensions only
- FileBrowserScreen: modal with the tree, a status bar showing the
  highlighted path, and Select/Cancel buttons; Enter on a file
  auto-confirms, Escape cancels
- action_browse routes to FileBrowserScreen on Linux and keeps the
  native OS dialog path (background thread) for macOS / Windows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(editor): improve list indentation and Enter key behavior (#3) * fix(editor): improve list indentation and Enter key behavior - Fix Enter on empty line inserting extra newline (\n\n\n → \n\n) - Fix Tab/Shift+Tab not applying to
continuation lines - Enable Tab on
continuation lines to change list hierarchy - Limit list indent to parent+1 level (sibling check) - Include child list items when indenting/outdenting parent - Fix unexpected range selection after Tab operation - Fix canIndentLine incorrectly skipping deeper items when searching upward - Fix child items indenting even when parent cannot be indented Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix(editor): address code review concerns - Guard against baseIndent=Infinity when no list lines found in initial block - Remove dead code: cursorDelta assignment in outdent continuation branch (i===0 never occurs there) - Bump version to 1.1.1 Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * chore: bump version to 1.1.1 in all config files Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix(editor): remove dead code in indent continuation branch Mirror of the same fix applied to the outdent branch: lines[0] is always a list marker line, so the continuation handler never executes at i===0. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
Add agent panel, agent-session linkage, and comprehensive test coverage

Agent system:
- AgentProvider abstract interface with versioned API
- AgentFleetProvider reads goals from YAML, state from filesystem
- AgentManagerPanel with tree widget, context menus, and search filter
- Sidebar uses QTabWidget wrapping [Sessions][Agents] tabs

Agent-session linkage:
- AgentSessionLinker coordinates between session and agent panels
- Sessions from agent attach show agent badge in tree
- Agent panel shows session state (Active/Detached/no session) as child nodes
- Tab titles for agent sessions show agent name instead of just directory
- "Show Agent" context menu on sessions, "Focus Tab"/"Reattach" on agents
- SessionMetadata.agentId persisted to/from JSON

Additional improvements:
- KonsolaiSettings: agent fleet path configuration
- Session filter also matches sessionId
- Docs: handbook pages for agents, sessions, yolo, budget, testing
- GUI smoke tests for agent panel and session panel

Tests (11 new test files):
- AgentFleetProviderTest, AgentManagerPanelTest, AgentSessionLinkerTest
- ClaudeProcessHookEventTest, ClaudeHookHandlerDetailTest, HookCleanupRaceTest
- KonsolaiSettingsTest, PromptTemplateManagerTest, TokenTrackingTest
- RemoteSshArgsTest, YoloPollingTest

commit.message:
fix(audit): R253-AUD-1 — fail-closed on metadata serialization failure

Previously, if serde_json::to_string() failed on metadata, the size
defaulted to 0 via unwrap_or(0), completely bypassing the 64KB
MAX_METADATA_SIZE check. Now returns AuditError::Validation on
serialization failure (fail-closed).

1 test added verifying oversized metadata is rejected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Read BUILD_TAG from .tag file instead of Docker ARG/ENV

Docker BuildKit was caching the ARG/ENV layer, causing stale build
tags. The .tag file is already written fresh by the GHA workflow
before docker build and copied into the image, so read it directly.

commit.message:
fix(parser): emit empty sublocation blocks so all sublocations register in metadata

Previously flushEntries() skipped blocks with zero entries, causing
sublocations like "שבלול 6" to be dropped if they had no carts under them.
Now empty blocks are emitted so _buildMapDB registers their sublocations.
